description: RFC #531 follow-on (value-first step 3): give `oh update` a DECLARED payload via `.oh/manifest.json` so it overlays an allowlist of `.oh/` content instead of all of `.oh/`, excluding `.oh/docs/` (a Docusaurus site) and `.oh/patches/` (a repo-specific dep patch) by omission. Stacks on PR #336 (feat/531-oh-update): it EXTENDS the runUpdate overlay introduced there. The `.oh/`-only path-escape guard (assertDestInTarget) is PRESERVED unchanged — manifest paths are relative to `.oh/`, so the manifest cannot reach outside `.oh/` (cross-tree `.mifune/skills` shipping is DEFERRED, decision C). Plus a guard probe locking the de-hardcode residual: shipped `.oh/` shell scripts must keep the upstream repo OVERRIDABLE (the `${AUTOPILOT_REPO:-…}` form), never a bare non-overridable literal. Five stories own DISJOINT files and run as one EDIT-only /delegate wave; the Advisor integrates, runs the gates (npm ci, node build.mjs, tsc --noEmit, full vitest, eval-probe suite, grep sweep), and commits per story. Base is origin/feat/531-oh-update.

userStories.title: Pure manifest matcher + loader in .oh/cli/src/lib/manifest.ts
userStories.description: As `oh update`, I need a zero-dependency, fully-testable matcher that decides whether a `.oh/`-relative relpath belongs in the declared payload, plus a loader for `.oh/manifest.json`.
acceptanceCriteria: Create NEW file .oh/cli/src/lib/manifest.ts (create the lib/ dir). Use node:fs (readFileSync) and node:path ONLY — no new dependency, mirroring commands/update.ts's import style. The whole module is PURE: no process.exit/argv/cwd, no writes.
Export `export interface Manifest { include: string[]; exclude: string[]; }`.

Export `export function globToRegExp(glob: string): RegExp` with these EXACT, documented semantics (US-003 unit-tests them directly): anchor the whole pattern with ^...$; `**` matches any run of characters INCLUDING '/' (cross-segment); a leading `**/` ALSO matches zero leading segments — implement by translating the `**/` token to `(?:.*/)?`; a standalone `**` (not followed by '/') translates to `.*`; a single `*` matches any characters EXCEPT '/' i.e. `[^/]*`; every regex-special character among `.+?^${}()|[]\` is backslash-escaped; a literal '/' stays '/'. A pattern with NO wildcard is therefore an EXACT match (e.g. `README.md` → /^README\.md$/, which matches 'README.md' but NOT 'cli/README.md'). Process the glob left-to-right so the two-char `**` token is detected before the one-char `*` (e.g. via an index walk, not sequential String.replace that could double-translate).

Export `export function shouldShip(relpath: string, manifest: Manifest): boolean`: returns true IFF `relpath` matches at least one `manifest.include` pattern AND zero `manifest.exclude` patterns (exclude WINS over include). `relpath` is always a POSIX '/'-separated path relative to `.oh/` (e.g. 'cli/src/cli.ts', 'docs/intro.md', 'manifest.json'). Build each pattern's RegExp via globToRegExp.

Export `export function loadManifest(fromOh: string): Manifest | null`: read `path.resolve(fromOh, 'manifest.json')` via readFileSync+JSON.parse inside try/catch. Return null (→ caller back-compat legacy-mode) when the file is ABSENT, unparseable, its parsed value lacks an array `include`, OR `include` is an EMPTY array. (An empty allowlist would make `shouldShip` return false for everything and silently ship NOTHING — a hollow-out footgun; treating it as 'no manifest' instead surfaces the legacy-mode warning. This empty-array → null rule is REQUIRED.) On success return `{ include: parsed.include, exclude: Array.isArray(parsed.exclude) ? parsed.exclude : [] }` (tolerate a missing `exclude` as []). Never throw.

Self-verify by re-reading the file for obvious TS/syntax issues (do NOT run tsc — the worktree has no .oh/cli/node_modules yet). Pin the exact exported names/signatures above; US-002 imports `loadManifest`/`shouldShip` and US-003 imports all four (incl. `globToRegExp`, `Manifest`).
Owns ONLY .oh/cli/src/lib/manifest.ts (new). Does NOT edit update.ts, cli.ts, the test, probes, manifest.json, RESULTS.md, README, or CHANGELOG.
userStories.notes: Worked example for the worker: globToRegExp('cli/**') → /^cli\/.*$/ (matches 'cli/src/cli.ts'); globToRegExp('**/node_modules/**') → /^(?:.*\/)?node_modules\/.*$/ (matches BOTH 'node_modules/x' and 'cli/node_modules/x'); globToRegExp('manifest.json') → /^manifest\.json$/. shouldShip('docs/intro.md', {include:['cli/**','README.md'],exclude:[]}) → false; shouldShip('cli/dist/oh.js', {include:['cli/**'],exclude:['**/dist/**']}) → false (exclude wins).

userStories.title: runUpdate honors the manifest in commands/update.ts
userStories.description: As `oh update`, I overlay only the manifest-declared payload, with a back-compat path when no manifest is present — without weakening the path-escape guard or changing the runUpdate signature.
acceptanceCriteria: Edit .oh/cli/src/commands/update.ts ONLY. Add `import { loadManifest, shouldShip } from '../lib/manifest.js';` (NodeNext .js specifier resolving to lib/manifest.ts) alongside the existing imports.

After the existing `walkFiles(fromOh, fromOh, relpaths); relpaths.sort();` (currently ~line 159-160) and BEFORE the overlay loop, add `const manifest = loadManifest(fromOh);`. If `manifest === null`, emit EXACTLY ONE line `io.stdout(dryPrefix + 'oh update: no .oh/manifest.json in source; overlaying all of .oh/ (legacy mode)\n');` (back-compat: behavior is then identical to today).

Inside the `for (const rel of relpaths)` loop, AFTER the existing per-segment node_modules/dist volatile-skip block (the `if (segments.includes('node_modules') || segments.includes('dist'))` continue) and BEFORE `const dest = path.resolve(targetOh, rel);`, insert: `if (manifest && !shouldShip(rel, manifest)) { io.stdout(dryPrefix + 'skip ' + rel + ' (not in payload)\n'); skipped++; continue; }`. This filters out docs/patches (and anything not allow-listed) while counting them in the existing `skipped` tally and the unchanged summary line.

Do NOT change the runUpdate/UpdateOptions/UpdateIO signatures, the preconditions, the version gate, the assertDestInTarget guard (keep it EXACTLY — it is the non-negotiable safety invariant), the volatile-skip, the dry-run write-suppression, or the final `oh update: <x> created, <y> overwritten, <s> skipped` summary line format. The manifest filter is purely additive between the volatile skip and the guard.

`npm --prefix .oh/cli ci` then `node .oh/cli/build.mjs` must bundle clean and `cd .oh/cli && npx tsc --noEmit` must pass (the Advisor runs these at integration; the worker only static-verifies by re-reading).
Owns ONLY .oh/cli/src/commands/update.ts. Does NOT create lib/manifest.ts (US-001), the test, probes, manifest.json, or docs.
userStories.notes: The import + the two inserted blocks are the WHOLE change. Depends on US-001's pinned `loadManifest`/`shouldShip` signatures — the Advisor's post-integration build+typecheck+vitest prove they agree.

userStories.title: Vitest suite .oh/cli/src/__tests__/manifest.test.ts
userStories.description: As CI's Test job, I need the matcher semantics and the end-to-end payload filtering (docs/patches excluded; back-compat) exhaustively covered.
acceptanceCriteria: Create NEW file .oh/cli/src/__tests__/manifest.test.ts (vitest; import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'). Auto-discovered by vitest.config.ts's `.oh/cli/**/__tests__/**/*.test.ts` include. Import `import { globToRegExp, shouldShip, loadManifest } from '../lib/manifest.js';` and `import { runUpdate } from '../commands/update.js';`.

MATCHER unit cases (each its own it()): (1) `cli/**` matches 'cli/src/cli.ts' and 'cli/x.ts' but NOT 'scripts/x.sh' nor 'clix' nor 'cli' (bare). (2) exact literal: `README.md` matches 'README.md' but NOT 'cli/README.md'; `manifest.json` matches 'manifest.json' only. (3) leading `**/`: `**/node_modules/**` matches BOTH 'node_modules/x' (zero leading segments) and 'cli/node_modules/pkg/i.js'. (4) single `*` is segment-bounded: globToRegExp('cli/*.ts') matches 'cli/a.ts' but NOT 'cli/sub/a.ts'. (5) shouldShip exclude-wins: shouldShip('cli/dist/oh.js', {include:['cli/**'], exclude:['**/dist/**']}) === false; shouldShip('cli/src/cli.ts', same) === true; shouldShip('docs/intro.md', {include:['cli/**','README.md'], exclude:[]}) === false.

loadManifest cases: a real temp dir with manifest.json present → returns the parsed {include,exclude}; manifest.json absent → null; manifest.json with invalid JSON → null; manifest.json present but no `include` array → null; manifest.json with an EMPTY `include: []` → null (empty allowlist treated as no-manifest — the hollow-out guard); manifest.json with include but no exclude → {include, exclude: []}. Build fixtures under node:os tmpdir + mkdtempSync; rmSync in afterEach.

INTEGRATION (the headline contract): build a fake NEWER source checkout `<src>/.oh/` containing `cli/package.json` (version e.g. '9.9.9') + `manifest.json` with include ['cli/**','README.md','manifest.json'] exclude ['**/dist/**'] + files `cli/cli.ts`, `cli/dist/oh.js`, `README.md`, `docs/site.md`, `patches/p.diff`; and a fake equipped target `<tgt>/.oh/cli/package.json` (version '0.1.0') plus a PROJECT file `<tgt>/harness.yaml`. Run `await runUpdate({targetDir:<tgt>, fromDir:<src>}, io)`. Assert rc 0 AND: `<tgt>/.oh/cli/cli.ts` and `<tgt>/.oh/README.md` and `<tgt>/.oh/manifest.json` now EXIST; `<tgt>/.oh/docs/site.md`, `<tgt>/.oh/patches/p.diff`, and `<tgt>/.oh/cli/dist/oh.js` were NOT created (existsSync false); `<tgt>/harness.yaml` is byte-identical (project untouched); and some stdout line includes 'skip docs/site.md (not in payload)'. Collect io via arrays.
BACK-COMPAT integration: same as above but the source `.oh/` has NO manifest.json. Assert rc 0, `<tgt>/.oh/docs/site.md` IS created (overlay-all), and a stdout line includes 'legacy mode'.

Run `/home/sandbox/harness/node_modules/.bin/vitest run --root <worktree>` (worktree has no node_modules; resolution walks up to repo-root node_modules) — your new file PLUS all existing suites (incl. update.test.ts) stay green. Self-verify before reporting PASS.
Owns ONLY .oh/cli/src/__tests__/manifest.test.ts (new). Does NOT edit lib/manifest.ts, update.ts, cli.ts, or any other file.
userStories.notes: Use mkdtempSync for isolation and build fixture trees with explicit writeFileSync/mkdirSync; never read the real repo .oh/. The integration cases are the proof that docs/patches stop shipping AND that back-compat is intact.

userStories.title: Production manifest + two eval probes + RESULTS rows
userStories.description: As CI's eval gate and the OpenHarness payload contract, I need the real `.oh/manifest.json`, a probe locking the manifest contract, and a probe locking the de-hardcode override seam.
acceptanceCriteria: Create NEW .oh/manifest.json — the production allowlist, valid JSON, EXACTLY: include = ["cli/**", "scripts/**", "install/**", "templates/**", "README.md", "manifest.json"]; exclude = ["**/node_modules/**", "**/dist/**"]. (docs/** and patches/** are OMITTED → not shipped. templates/** is listed preemptively for when PR #334 adds it; it matches nothing on this base, harmlessly.) Pretty-print with 2-space indent + trailing newline.

Create NEW evals/probes/oh-payload-manifest.sh — 3-state oracle (exit 0 PASS / 1 REGRESSION / 2 SKIPPED). Header EXACTLY three comment lines: `# tier: A`, `# source: issue #531 follow-on (.oh payload manifest — oh update ships a declared allowlist)`, `# desc: oh update overlays only manifest-declared .oh payload (docs/patches excluded); static guard that the manifest + matcher + integration are wired.`. `set -euo pipefail`; `ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)"`. SKIPPED(2, stderr 'SKIPPED .oh payload manifest not present') if `$ROOT/.oh/manifest.json` is absent. Else PASS(0) requires ALL (each failure → exit 1, stderr 'REGRESSION <reason>'): manifest.json parses as JSON with a NON-EMPTY `include` array (use `jq -e 'has("include") and (.include|type=="array") and (.include|length>0)' "$ROOT/.oh/manifest.json"`); the include list does NOT contain `docs/**` nor `patches/**` (`jq -e '.include | index("docs/**") | not'` and same for patches/**); `$ROOT/.oh/cli/src/lib/manifest.ts` exists and contains `export function shouldShip` and `export function loadManifest`; `$ROOT/.oh/cli/src/commands/update.ts` imports from `../lib/manifest.js` (grep -q) AND contains the literal `(not in payload)` skip marker (grep -q).

Create NEW evals/probes/oh-shipped-repo-overridable.sh — 3-state oracle locking the de-hardcode residual. Header: `# tier: A`, `# source: issue #531 follow-on (de-hardcode residual — shipped .oh shell scripts keep the upstream repo overridable)`, `# desc: STATIC guard (.sh-scoped) — no SHIPPED .oh shell script directly ASSIGNS a bare upstream-repo literal to a variable; it must use the overridable \${VAR:-…} form. Comparisons/help-text are fine. Real config-derivation design lives in the autopilot skill + cron-runtime; shipped .ts/.mjs are clean today + covered by typecheck.`. `set -euo pipefail`; same ROOT idiom. SKIPPED(2, stderr 'SKIPPED .oh/scripts/install.sh not present') if `$ROOT/.oh/scripts/install.sh` is absent. Else use a WHITELIST heuristic (no fragile quote-in-regex): collect every shipped-.sh line mentioning the upstream repo, then drop the four LEGITIMATE forms and flag the rest — `hits=$(grep -rn 'mifunedev/openharness\|ryaneggz/openharness' "$ROOT/.oh" --include='*.sh' 2>/dev/null || true)` then `bad=$(printf '%s\n' "$hits" | grep -v ':-' | grep -vE ':[0-9]+:[[:space:]]*#' | grep -v '!=' | grep -v 'default:' || true)`. The four whitelisted forms are: the bash default-override `:-` (overridable), a `#` comment, a `!=`/test comparison, and `default:` help-text. VERIFIED against this base — install.sh:221 `${OH_GITHUB_REPO:-…}` (has `:-`), install.sh:135 `(default: …)` (has `default:`), install.sh:225 `!= "…"` (has `!=`), restart-openharness-tmux.sh:29 `${OH_REPO:-…}` (has `:-`) are ALL whitelisted → `bad` empty → PASS. Any OTHER occurrence (a bare `REPO="mifunedev/openharness"` assignment, an executable `--repo mifunedev/openharness`) survives all four filters → flagged. If `bad` is non-empty → exit 1 stderr `REGRESSION non-overridable upstream-repo reference in shipped .oh script: <bad>`. Then POSITIVELY assert the known override is intact: `grep -q '${OH_GITHUB_REPO:-' "$ROOT/.oh/scripts/install.sh"` else exit 1 'REGRESSION install.sh lost the OH_GITHUB_REPO override'. PASS(0) otherwise. Self-verify it exits 0 on this base.

Both probes shellcheck-clean (`shellcheck evals/probes/oh-payload-manifest.sh evals/probes/oh-shipped-repo-overridable.sh` exits 0 at default level) and exit 0 when run against the built worktree. Self-verify both before reporting PASS.

Insert TWO new rows into evals/RESULTS.md in their correct ALPHABETICAL slot by probe id: `| oh-payload-manifest | A | 2026-06-27 00:00 | PASS | issue #531 follow-on (.oh payload manifest) 2026-06-27 |` and `| oh-shipped-repo-overridable | A | 2026-06-27 00:00 | PASS | issue #531 follow-on (de-hardcode residual) 2026-06-27 |`. Both sort BEFORE the existing `oh-update` row (p < s < u): insert them BETWEEN the `next-dev-prod` row and the `oh-update` row, in the order `oh-payload-manifest` then `oh-shipped-repo-overridable`. Match the existing column shape EXACTLY; do NOT alter any other row.

Owns ONLY .oh/manifest.json (new), evals/probes/oh-payload-manifest.sh (new), evals/probes/oh-shipped-repo-overridable.sh (new), and evals/RESULTS.md. Does NOT touch the cli, lib, tests, README, or CHANGELOG.
userStories.notes: The manifest probe may use jq -e (CI has jq; the eval runner uses it elsewhere); if unsure, fall back to grep-based JSON checks but prefer jq. The override-guard probe is a WHITELIST heuristic (`:-`/`#`/`!=`/`default:`) verified clean against this base — `prompt-miner-caps.sh` is NOT in `.oh/` (it lives at `.mifune/skills/prompt-miner/`, not shipped by `oh update`); the actual shipped refs are `install.sh` + `maintenance/restart-openharness-tmux.sh`, both whitelisted. Run BOTH probes and confirm exit 0 BEFORE writing the PASS rows (do not pre-seed a PASS that the probe contradicts).

userStories.title: Doc the payload boundary: .oh/README.md + CHANGELOG
userStories.description: As OpenHarness, the payload manifest must be documented and the change recorded in the changelog.
acceptanceCriteria: Edit .oh/README.md: add a concise `## Payload manifest` section (or fold into the existing `oh update` section) documenting: that `oh update` overlays a DECLARED allowlist from `.oh/manifest.json` (paths relative to `.oh/`), not all of `.oh/`; that `.oh/docs/` (the docs site) and `.oh/patches/` are intentionally EXCLUDED (omitted from `include`) so they are not vendored into consumer repos; that the manifest itself ships so its policy propagates forward; the back-compat rule (no manifest in source → overlay-all 'legacy mode' with a warning); and that the manifest CANNOT reach outside `.oh/` (the path-escape guard is preserved; cross-tree shipping of `.mifune/skills` is deferred). Note the `oh init` seam briefly (init does not yet honor the manifest; `commands/init.ts`/`.oh/templates/` ship in PR #334) WITHOUT creating or importing those files. Preserve the rest of the README.

Add a CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entry under ### Added: 'Add `.oh/manifest.json` payload manifest honored by `oh update` (RFC #531 follow-on): `oh update` now overlays a declared allowlist of `.oh/` content and no longer vendors `.oh/docs/` or `.oh/patches/` into equipped repos; missing-manifest sources fall back to legacy overlay-all. Plus a guard probe keeping shipped `.oh` scripts' upstream-repo reference overridable.' Link the FULL URL https://github.com/mifunedev/openharness/issues/531 (NOT bare #531). If no [Unreleased] section exists, add one at the top in the file's existing Keep-a-Changelog style.

Do NOT edit any code, test, probe, manifest.json, or RESULTS.md. Keep claims accurate (no remote-fetch, no cross-tree shipping, no instance extraction). Do NOT document the manifest as reaching outside `.oh/`.
Owns ONLY .oh/README.md and CHANGELOG.md. Does NOT touch anything else.
userStories.notes: If CHANGELOG already has an [Unreleased] block (it may, from #336's entry on this stacked base), append within it; do not create a duplicate or disturb #336's entry.
